rationale.R1.2: The draft contains a Lagrangian derivation, a binomial-tail probability chain, and a monotonicity claim (k* non-decreasing in p-bar when p-bar > 1/2); logical consistency of these intermediates and their connection to the stated conclusion is a natural and high-stakes improvement target in any technical draft.
rationale.R1.4: The draft makes two explicit 'first to our knowledge' novelty claims — one for the optimization-theoretic framing and one for the counter-intuitive monotonicity prescription — against a named set of prior works (Condorcet, Grofman, Hansen-Salamon, Schapire); verifying or tightening these deltas is central to improving academic quality.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation 'improve this draft' most commonly signals prose-level work; several passages have mixed register (informal 'p-bar', clashing citation styles, run-on subordinate clauses) that standard draft improvement would address.
rationale.R1.7: Six specific citations are present and load-bearing for both the background narrative and the novelty claims; accuracy of years, author lists, and coverage of omitted relevant work (e.g., mixture-of-experts ensemble literature) is improvement-relevant.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement include checking technical and mathematical correctness of the derivations and the accuracy of the novelty claims, or is the scope limited to writing clarity and prose style only?
